Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log in DuckDB with columns case_id activity and timestamp We need to filter out cases groups of events by case_id that have more than 6 events So we want to keep only the events that belong to case_ids that have 6 or fewer events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps 1 First we can compute the number of events per case_id 2 Then we can use this to filter the original table to only include rows with case_ids that have a count <= 6

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We can do this in two ways Option 1 Use a subquery to get the case_ids that meet the condition and then join or use IN Option 2 Use a window function to count the number of events per case_id and then filter

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Option 1 using a subquery with GROUP BY and HAVING SELECT FROM event_log WHERE case_id IN SELECT case_id FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT <= 6

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Option 2 using a window function We can compute the count per case_id and then filter in an outer query WITH counts AS SELECT COUNT OVER PARTITION BY case_id as event_count FROM event_log SELECT FROM counts WHERE event_count <= 6

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: However note that the window function method may be more efficient in some databases if we have an index on case_id but in DuckDB its a columnar database and both might be efficient

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Since we are only interested in the original rows and the condition is on the count of events per case the subquery method might be more straightforward

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We must group by case_id to count and then use that condition

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: But wait what if there are NULL case_ids The problem does not specify but typically case_id should not be NULL We assume case_id is non null

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Alternatively we can also use a Common Table Expression for clarity Heres the query with a CTE for the counts WITH case_counts AS SELECT case_id COUNT as event_count FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id SELECT el FROM event_log el JOIN case_counts cc ON el case_id = cc case_id WHERE cc event_count <= 6

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: I will choose the subquery method with IN because its simple and does not require a join

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The problem says filter out any cases containing more than six events meaning we remove cases with >6 events So we want to keep cases with <=6 events

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This should return the events for cases with 6 or fewer events

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me test with a simple example Suppose we have case_id 1 with 2 events case_id 2 with 5 events and case_id 3 with 7 events The query should return all events from case_id 1 and 2 Then the IN clause will include 1 and 2 So the query is correct

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are using the condition in the subquery to filter the case_ids then selecting all columns for those case_ids So the query is SELECT FROM event_log WHERE case_id IN SELECT case_id FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT <= 6
